discussion_comments.comment_body:
Ah yes, TSan did not report it but indeed it has data race. I agree on the `pending_read` change, but I think `atomicSetWithSync(c->pending_ack, 0);` is bad here as in the context of no IO threads this is unnecessary atomic operation. 

EDIT: Can't think of a better place to put it though. So I think best to check
`else if (server.io_threads_num > 1) ...`

> else if (server.io_threads_num > 1) ...

looks good to me

is it needed to call statsUpdateActiveClients() when this client returns to the main thread in processClientsFromIOThread()?
Even to prevent the client from never having a complete command, it is possible to update whether it is active in IOThreadClientsCron()?

I have intentionally called `statsUpdateActiveClients(c)` from the main-thread (from `processInputBuffer()`) to avoid races on the shared sliding-window state (`active_clients_window[]` / `active_clients_window_ts`).
A separate call in `processClientsFromIOThread()` is not needed for complete-command traffic, because IO threads mark CLIENT_IO_PENDING_COMMAND and transfer the client to main thread, which then executes `processPendingCommandAndInputBuffer()` and reaches the same `processInputBuffer()` call site.

I think that using `IOThreadClientsCron()` for this metric would be incorrect, since cron marks clients by periodic scheduling (including idle clients), not by read activity, and would overcount `active_clients`.

I am aware that clients that continuously send incomplete input and never form a complete command may be undercounted in `active_clients`, but such behaviour seems to me like a pathological pattern and I suggest leaving it as is.

WDYT?

You are correct.
I have submitted a fix with a new test. Without the fix the test reproduces the bug.
Please review

why duplicate the object rather than find it in the db and detach it so the db won't free it?
i.e. we wanna decrement the refcount from the main thread, in theory for all objects, but it's (probably) easier to run through all clients and their replies rather than run through all objects in the db.

p.s. even running through all clients could be terrible (i've seen cases with some 30k clients), maybe we can keep a list of clients with pending replies?

ohh, silly me, we can't lookup the object in the db (we don't have a key), but better yet, we don't need a lookup.
if we have just two refs, we can easily nullify the `ptr` of the original robj, and let the reply object destroy it when it's done (i.e. dup the robj, let each have refcount of 1, and only one of them hold the string, the other will hold NULL).
if we have more than two refs, then i'm not sure what to do.

another idea is to remove the refcount from the robj, and add it using #14445 only for large keys. i.e. when the value is small, we copy it and we don't need the refcount, and when it's large, we don't care to spend 8 more bytes.
then the refcount can probably be a proper atomic integer.
i'm not sure i like this idea, but putting it here as an alternative, maybe someone can further improve it.

> if we have just two refs, we can easily nullify the `ptr` of the original robj, and let the reply object destroy it when it's done (i.e. dup the robj, let each have refcount of 1, and only one of them hold the string, the other will hold NULL). if we have more than two refs, then i'm not sure what to do.

a robj might be referenced by multi clients, so this way won't work.
yes, i realized that. so either we fall back to copying in that case, or if we wanna solve it better, we should add an atomic refcount like i mentioned in my previous post (only for large objects).
@oranagra yes, atomic is the simplest way, let's wait for #14445 and then continue this.
if we want, we don't have to wait. just choose between copy and taking ownership, depending on the value of the refcount.

I think we need to use the returned value here to avoid double free due to race.
Say if `o->refcount == 1`, two threads enter `decrRefCount()` and `atomicGet()`  `refcount == 1`, would double free (though two `decrRefCount()`s with refcount of 1 is def a bug).

```suggestion
    refcount = atomicDecr(o->refcount, 1);
```
good catch.
done with https://github.com/redis/redis/pull/14608/commits/517efcca90d8f1f4cc21580304534639297bcf5b

don't we reset `io_deferred_objects_num` and `io_deferred_objects_size`?
i think we may call `freeClientIODeferredObjects` multiple times
besides, we maybe we can call it in `keepClientInMainThread` to avoid necessary memory cost

> don't we reset `io_deferred_objects_num` and `io_deferred_objects_size`? i think we may call `freeClientIODeferredObjects` multiple times

I originally assumed that it wouldn't be called repeatedly, but let's add it anyway.

> besides, we maybe we can call it in `keepClientInMainThread` to avoid necessary memory cost

make sense.
done with https://github.com/redis/redis/pull/14608/commits/7fd2b547e0366de9dd428bc6dff852c0d9129bb1

Maybe I am being a bit nitpicky, but I still feel this should be split into two functions, a new function to remove client from pending referenced reply clients list if needed. Even though they are often called at similar times, that’s not relevant, the client still has referenced reply even when we should free io deferred objects. And we can call this function separately in `writeToClient` and `protectClientReplyObjects`. WDYT @oranagra
done with https://github.com/redis/redis/pull/14608/commits/440dc42f936116b530e086bec8b538f6281e2b8b, but only tryUnlinkClientFromPendingRefReply () is to reuse
In https://github.com/redis/redis/pull/14608, decrRefCount may come from io threads, so this callback must ensure it's thread safe.

yes. The KeyMeta code itself is only rely on kvobj bits and `keyMetaClass[keyMetaId].conf.free`. I added const on `keyMetaClass[keyMetaId].conf` to be on the safe side. and also updated module doc of `free` to include the comment `This callback may run in a background thread and is not protected by GIL.` (nothing in mentioned when i look on free of  module data type. maybe we should comment there as well?!)
cool, now we don't need call same functions that are called in `keepClientInMainThread`.

i wonder why did you add the condition?
same for CLIENT_ASKING and CLIENT_REPLY_SKIP below.

from my perspective this looks like blame log damage and unnecessary possible conflict.
It was because of TSan - it catches a data race on flags between main and IO thread(even though there isnt one). These checks put TSan’s mind at rest
